checks_performed: Blind protocol: verifying instance received only the charter criterion and raw source texts; no prior classification visible (freeze commits dab655c, f4136f1)

conflicts_of_interest: the same responsible human (the owner) stands behind proposer, executor, and verifier instances; independence here is substantive (blind primary-source re-reading), not institutional.

independence_profile.code_path: no pipeline code executed — manual blind protocol per task-packets/K1-T06.yaml (verification, not replication; MTH-014 boundary respected)
independence_profile.data_access_path: brief-embedded material only; the blind instance had no repository, database, or web access

independence_profile.prompt_family: blind verification brief (corpora/model-collapse/verification/blind-brief.md), frozen commit dab655c — a lineage distinct from every pipeline prompt

rationale: All three paper abstracts, re-fetched from arXiv on 2026-07-22 and read blind, support that the model-collapse mechanism exists as described: Shumailov et al. name and demonstrate it across model classes; Alemohammad et al. confirm degradation absent sufficient fresh real data (a scoping refinement, not a contradiction); Gerstgrasser et al. explicitly confirm collapse under data replacement while showing accumulation avoids it — a scope qualification on inevitability, not a refutation of existence. 3/3 agreement with the pipeline's supports relations.
reviewer_role: blind primary-source verifier (K1-T06, candidate c of the 2026-07-22 owner decision)
